reasoning: Google Chrome holds an overwhelming lead in the browser market, consistently hovering around 65% to 72% across all platforms according to recent StatCounter and other market share reports up to October 2025. The second most popular browser, Safari, is roughly around 18%, leaving a gap that cannot realistically be closed by the end of 2025. No events or developments exist that suggest Chrome will lose its top position on Wikipedia's 'Usage share of web browsers' table before December 31, 2025. Therefore, it is virtually certain that Chrome will remain the most popular browser. To obey the maximum bound limit, the assigned probability is 0.95.
